items.title: Ukraine slams Russian 'cynicism' ahead of separate truces
items.summary: KYIV: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Tuesday accused Russia of "utter cynicism" for launching deadly attacks while also seeking a truce to stage its May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow. Several people were killed in overnight strikes that came after both Moscow and Kyiv announced unilateral ceasefires over different dates this week. Moscow has announced a ceasefire during public World War II celebrations over May 9, and Kyiv has said it will halt fire over May 6.

items.title: Affin Bank, Sime Darby rev up premium banking push with Porsche campaign
items.summary: AFFIN Bank Bhd has partnered with Sime Darby Auto Performance, the Porsche distributor in Malaysia, to launch the "Race to the Extraordinary" campaign as part of its push to strengthen its premium and private banking proposition. It will be anchored by access to the Porsche Carrera Cup Asia and the return of the Porsche Esports Sprint Challenge Malaysia. Affin group president and group chief executive officer Datuk Wan Razly Abdullah said the partnership reflects the bank's ambition to deliver differentiated client experiences beyond traditional banking services.

items.title: Media organisations encouraged to utilise RM30mil innovation fund - Teo
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Media organisations are encouraged to apply for the RM30 million Media Innovation Fund, which is now entering its fifth phase, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said. Elaborating, Teo said the fund was introduced to support the media industry amid mounting challenges driven by shifts in the advertising landscape. "We hope this fund can assist our media industry, especially as advertising revenues are increasingly captured by social media platforms," she said.

items.title: Israel court extends detention of two Gaza flotilla activists to Sunday: rights group
items.summary: ASHKELON, Israel: An Israeli court has extended the detention of two foreign activists from a Gaza-bound flotilla until Sunday, a rights group representing them said, as authorities continue to question the pair. Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avila appeared before a court in the city of Ashkelon for their second hearing on Tuesday, after they were brought to Israel for questioning last week. "The court approved their detention until Sunday morning," Miriam Azem, international advocacy coordinator at the Israeli rights group Adalah told AFP.

items.title: AI expected to contribute up to RM20b yearly to Malaysia’s GDP by 2030, says Gobind
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 5 — Artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to contribute between RM13 billion and RM20 billion annually to Malaysia’s gross domestic product (GDP) by 2030, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo. He said AI is already demonstrating tangible economic value in Malaysia, with projections indicating it could add between 0.8 and 1.2 percentage points to Malaysia’s GDP growth each year. “At the national level, the digital economy, increasingly powered by AI, contributed over 23 per cent to Malaysia’s GDP last year, amounting to more than RM450 billion in economic value.

items.title: Fuel station attendant fined RM10,000 for misusing MyKads to buy subsidised petrol
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Bangladeshi petrol pump attendant was fined RM10,000 by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to misusing two MyKads to purchase the subsidised Budi Madani Ron95 petrol last month. The offences were committed between February 8 and April 22, and between April 21 and 22, at a petrol station along Jalan Pahang. "The court must consider the losses incurred as the victims lost their subsidised petrol quota.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia closes higher on late buying
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia reversed earlier losses to end higher on Tuesday as buying interest gained momentum in the afternoon session, said an analyst. At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 7.66 points, or 0.44 per cent, to close at 1,747.43 from Monday's close of 1,739.77. Turnover decreased to 2.86 billion units worth RM2.59 billion compared with 3.05 billion units worth RM2.55 billion yesterday.

items.title: Scorching streaks: MetMalaysia warns Kelantan, Pahang and Sabah hit by Level One heatwave
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a Level One (Yellow) heatwave status for four areas in Peninsular Malaysia and one in Sabah as of 4pm today. MetMalaysia said the four areas are Jeli dan Kuala Krai in Kelantan, as well as Jerantut and Maran in Pahang, while the area in Sabah involves the district of Telupid. The Level One heatwave status is issued when an area records a maximum daily temperature of 35 to 37 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Mara weighs listing of two education arms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) is in the early stages of evaluating a potential listing of two of its higher education institutions. A structured decision framework is expected within next year with a possible timeline extending to 2028, Mara director-general Zulfikri Osman said. Zulfikri said the review involves Universiti Kuala Lumpur (UniKL) and Universiti Poly-Tech Malaysia (UPTM), which he described as Mara's strongest-performing education entities.

items.title: Landlords beware: Don't pay for your tenant's bitcoin mining—transfer your TNB account
items.summary: KUANTAN: Property owners are advised to transfer Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) accounts into their tenants' names when renting out properties. This move is aimed at preventing landlords from shouldering the burden of potential hefty electricity bills if their premises are used for illegal activities, including bitcoin mining. Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the change of account holder would protect landlords in cases where tenants make illegal electricity connections.

items.title: Two statutory body teaching staff remanded in Terengganu over alleged RM30,000 bribery case, says MACC
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 5 — Two employees of a statutory body in Terengganu have been remanded for allegedly soliciting and accepting bribes of around RM30,000 in return for issuing competency certificates, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Agency said today. An MACC source said both male suspects, in their 20s and 40s, who worked as teaching staff at the statutory body, were remanded for four days till May 8 today following their arrest yesterday when they showed up to provide their statements at the state MACC office. “Preliminary investigations revealed that both suspects allegedly colluded to commit the offence in 2025 and the bribes solicited were for issuing ‘immediate approval’ of competency certificates for every participant of the course.

items.title: PM directs air force to hasten cloud seeding ops in northern states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ordered the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) to expedite cloud seeding operations in northern Peninsular Malaysia following the prolonged dry spell. Anwar said the government has also instructed the Fire and Rescue Department to continue providing assistance in the ongoing operations over the next few days. Yesterday, Anwar also instructed the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) to speed up cloud seeding operations in states currently at critical levels, particularly Kedah and Perlis.

items.title: Malaysia studies taser use as first step to arming Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency, says home minister
items.summary: SEPANG, May 5 — The Home Ministry is studying a proposal to use electric stun guns (tasers) as an initial step in efforts to equip personnel of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) with weaponry. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said a policy decision has been agreed upon to provide weaponry for AKPS, with implementation to be carried out in the near future. He did stress that the government’s direction is clear towards equipping AKPS personnel with weaponry to strengthen border control capabilities, pointing out that border control duties involve high risks and require personnel who are psychologically prepared and well-trained.

items.title: Bertam Valley residents awarded RM4.7mil in damages over 2013 floods
items.summary: Lawyer M. Manogaran, representing the residents, said the quantum of damages was assessed today by judge Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Mohd Salleh at the Seremban High Court. Manogaran said the court ordered TNB to pay RM912,459.76 in special damages to the plaintiffs. He said the court also awarded RM30,000 in general damages to each plaintiff, and exemplary damages of RM10,000 to 83 plaintiffs.

items.title: Myanmar man charged with murder of ex-wife in Setapak condominium stabbing case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A Myanmar national was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of his ex-wife in front of a condominium block in Setapak last month. Aung Tun Ngwa, 26, who is unemployed, nodded in understanding after the charge was read out before Magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri. Aung was charged with murdering Merry Yet Ngwa, 33, also a Myanmar national, in front of Block 1 of Genting Court Condominium, Taman Setapak Indah Jaya, near here, at 2.30pm on April 23.

items.title: Govt approves RM200 per hectare advance aid for paddy farmers across Malaysia, says Anwar
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 5 — The government has agreed to provide an advance payment of RM200 per hectare under the Ploughing Incentive Special Aid to help padi farmers nationwide. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative, approved at today’s National Economic Action Council MTEN) meeting, is expected to benefit nearly 240,000 registered farmers, with a financial implication of RM48 million. Anwar said farmers’ concerns have also been addressed through the Ploughing Incentive for Padi Farmers (IPKP), announced earlier by Agriculture and Food Security Minister Mohamad Sabu on April 14.

items.title: Bitcoin mining electricity theft costs Malaysia RM700m a year, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 5 — Malaysia loses an estimated RM700 million annually due to electricity theft linked to illegal bitcoin mining, said Deputy Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad. He said efforts to curb the activity will be intensified nationwide to reduce losses and ensure fairness for consumers who pay their electricity bills. Abdul Rahman said such electricity theft is highly dangerous and poses a significant fire risk, noting that illegal cable connections that do not meet Energy Commission safety standards can cause electric shocks.

items.title: Anwar directs intensified cloud seeding operations in northern Peninsular Malaysia amid dry spell
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) and the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) to step up cloud seeding operations in northern Peninsular Malaysia in view of the hot and dry weather. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the operations must be carried out urgently. Yesterday, Anwar also instructed the Meteorological Department to expedite similar operations in critically affected states, particularly Kedah and Perlis.

items.title: Suspect in fatal Ketereh college student stabbing to be charged soon - Kelantan cops
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A suspect in the fatal stabbing of a college student in Ketereh last Friday, in which the victim was stabbed 61 times, is expected to be charged soon. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigations into the case are about 70 per cent complete. Another suspect who was previously remanded will be released on police bail.

items.title: 'Spy in a Cheongsam' Blossom Wong laid to rest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former police officer and undercover operative Blossom Wong Kooi Fong, famously known as the "Spy in a Cheongsam", was laid to rest today in a solemn and intimate ceremony attended by family members and close friends. Her funeral rites began with a two-day wake at Nirvana Memorial Park in Bukit Seputeh before the hearse departed at 11am for Nirvana Memorial Park in Shah Alam, where the cremation ceremony was held at noon. At the crematorium, her daughter, Dr Christina Blossom Welch, led family members in placing flowers on Wong's coffin as a final gesture of farewell.

items.title: Kedah police bust 'Geng Atan' loan shark syndicate, solve 11 cases
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Police have detained five suspects, including a married couple, believed to be members of the "Geng Atan" syndicate, for criminal intimidation in Sungai Petani. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said police received a report on April 16 from a 27-year-old man who claimed his vehicle was set on fire after he failed to repay an online loan. He said further investigations revealed the suspects acted as hired executors to deliver warning notes, splash paint, and burn victims' vehicles.

items.title: Fraud defence, SME financing top Public Bank's priorities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Public Bank Bhd says it will prioritise fraud prevention, digitalisation and small and medium enterprise (SME) financing in 2026. The bank will also seek to sustain growth through cross-selling and non-interest income expansion over the next three to five years. Managing director and chief executive officer Tan Sri Dr Tay Ah Lek said Public Bank remains cautious of global headwinds but expects domestic resilience to support its core businesses.

items.title: Police submit investigation paper on Melor assemblyman to DPP
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The investigation paper concerning allegations that Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud belittled police officers during a roadblock has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the paper was submitted last week for further action. "At the police investigation level, it is complete as it has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor.

items.title: Malaysia among most resilient emerging markets despite global shocks, says Moody’s Ratings report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Malaysia is among the most resilient emerging markets in navigating recent global financial shocks, supported by policy strength and relatively stable market conditions, according to Moody’s Ratings. In a report released today, the global rating agency said Malaysia, rated A3 with a stable outlook, was among several large emerging market economies demonstrating strong resilience across key market indicators. “Market access was preserved throughout, and stress was absorbed without prolonged disruption across market channels.

items.title: Police bust two international scam rings, seize fake ‘Delhi Police’ uniforms in KL raids
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Two online scam syndicates targeting victims in India and the United States were crippled in separate raids at a luxury condominium in the capital last month. He said the syndicate’s modus operandi involved impersonating Indian authorities and accusing victims of being involved in the dissemination of seditious content. “Most shockingly, the syndicate also used ‘Delhi Police’ uniforms bearing the name tag ‘Inspector Sunil Kumar’.

items.title: Loke: KLIA Aerotrain expected to resume 24-hour operations by end of May
items.summary: SEPANG: The Aerotrain service at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) is expected to resume full 24-hour operations by the end of May. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the restoration process is now in its final phase after Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) completed all necessary technical tests. He explained that a full report on the tests is being submitted to the Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) for evaluation and safety validation before full operations commence.

items.title: ‘Game-changer’: MyNIISe could cut airport clearance time to five seconds, says home minister
items.summary: SEPANG, May 5 — The National Integrated Immigration System (MyNIISe) at the country’s entry points this September is expected to cut passenger inspection times to between four and five seconds, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. The new system, estimated to cost some RM1 billion, replaces the Malaysian Immigration System (MyIMMs) after 20 years. Saifuddin Nasution had touted the MyNIISe as the catalyst to strengthening border control.

items.title: China fireworks factory explosion kills 26, injures 61
items.summary: BEIJING: The death toll from a giant explosion at a fireworks factory in central China rose to 26, with 61 more injured, officials said Tuesday. The explosion occurred at around 4.43pm on Monday at the Liuyang Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Company in Liuyang, Hunan province, state broadcaster CCTV reported. Following the blast, all fireworks makers in Hunan's provincial capital Changsha, which administers Liuyang, had been ordered to stop production ahead of safety inspections, CCTV said.

items.title: KLIA aerotrain set to resume 24-hour service by end-May, says Loke
items.summary: SEPANG, May 5 — The aerotrain service at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) is expected to resume 24-hour operations by the end of this month, pending approval from the relevant authorities, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. Elaborating further, Loke said the aerotrain is currently still in operation but is halted for a few hours at night to allow for maintenance and inspections. Previously, Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said that the KLIA Terminal 1 Aerotrain had recorded 19 incidents between July 2 and September 30, due to various issues, including human-related incidents such as passengers forcing train doors open.

items.title: Bukit Aman: Commercial crime cases up nearly 13pc in Q1 this year with over 64,000 reports
items.summary: Rusdi said 16,880 online scam cases were recorded in the first quarter of this year, involving losses of more than RM555.7 million, compared with 12,110 cases with losses of RM573.6 million last year. “It was followed by e-commerce crimes with 4,858 cases (losses exceeding RM30.4 million), non-existent investment scams with 2,204 cases (losses of RM246.7 million), and non-existent loan scams with 2,101 cases (losses of RM15.5 million). “Also recorded were e-financial crimes with 1,485 cases involving losses of RM83.4 million, as well as love scams with 251 cases and losses of RM6.6 million,” he said.

items.title: Commuters beware: MetMalaysia warns of heavy storms across Klang Valley and 10 states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an urgent weather warning for the federal territories and several states, with heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to disrupt the evening commute until 7.00 pm today Residents and commuters in the Klang Valley are advised to exercise extreme caution as the warning covers the entirety of Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, and Putrajaya. Coinciding with the peak evening rush hour, the downpour is expected to significantly impact traffic flow across major highways and urban arteries. In Perak, the warning extends across Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.

items.title: Johor government yet to discuss details of proposal to appoint five assemblymen, says exco
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 5 — A proposal to amend Johor state laws to allow the appointment of five unelected state assemblymen has yet to be discussed in detail, Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said today. Ling said the proposal, which would allow the state government to appoint five assemblymen to the Johor legislative assembly, was still at an early stage. Ling said an alternative proposal to redraw Johor’s electoral boundaries and increase the number of elected state seats would take longer, as the process would have to be taken to the federal level.

items.title: Johor to link schools directly to JS-SEZ industries under new learning ecosystem
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 5 — Johor is developing a learning ecosystem that directly links schools with industries in the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ). Johor Education and Information Committee chairman Aznan Tamin said the learning ecosystem involves the Corporate Adoption Programme and Industry Satellite Laboratory. Aznan said for students to have an interest in pure science, the teaching approach needs to be changed with the addition of Advanced Teacher Training and Applied Science Modules.

items.title: Anwar: Govt to expand Jualan Rahmah with weekly sales in all state constituencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The government will expand its Jualan Rahmah Madani initiative to be held weekly in every state constituency nationwide, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today. He said the move is part of broader efforts to ease cost-of-living pressures, particularly by ensuring consistent access to essential goods at lower prices. Last year, over 25,000 Jualan Rahmah events were held nationwide, covering all state constituencies.

items.title: Pakatan presidential council to meet ahead of talks with BN, says Saifuddin Nasution amid speculation over N. Sembilan
items.summary: SEPANG, May 5 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) presidential council will hold a meeting this evening before speaking to leaders of Barisan Nasional later today as the alliance between the two coalitions face a critical test in Negeri Sembilan. PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will chair the meeting in Putrajaya, as well as PKR’s political bureau meeting beforehand. Earlier today, Umno president and Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi gave his assurance that the party’s Negeri Sembilan assemblymen will remain in the state’s governing unity coalition.

items.title: Myanmar man charged with ex-wife's murder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Myanmar man was charged at the magistrate's court with the murder of his ex-wife two weeks ago. Aung Tun Ngwa, 26, nodded to indicate he understood the charge read out to him in Bahasa Malaysia before magistrate Izzah Hasan Basri. No plea was recorded as murder cases are under the jurisdiction of the High Court.

items.title: AI expected to contribute up to RM20bil annually to Malaysia's GDP by 2030 –Gobind Singh
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to contribute between RM13 billion and RM20 billion annually to Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) by 2030, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo. He said AI is already demonstrating tangible economic value in Malaysia, with projections indicating it could add between 0.8 and 1.2 percentage points to Malaysia's GDP growth each year. "At the national level, the digital economy, increasingly powered by AI, contributed over 23 per cent to Malaysia's GDP last year, amounting to more than RM450 billion in economic value.

items.title: Johor says JS-SEZ, RTS growth will not drive up cost of living
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor government has assured that rapid economic expansion under the Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) and Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) will not come at the expense of rising living costs. State Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said while both mega projects are expected to generate strong economic spillover, the state is mindful of potential pressure on food prices, rental rates and housing costs. Lee said the state's approach is to ensure growth remains inclusive, backed by targeted interventions that directly benefit daily life.

items.title: Anwar orders rent cuts at federal govt-linked premises to aid small traders, urges states and local councils to follow suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Putrajaya will lower rental rates at federal government-owned commercial premises from this month to ease cost pressures on small traders, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said the move would involve premises owned by government-linked entities and agencies, and urged state governments and local councils nationwide to adopt similar measures. The first step is to reduce rental rates.

items.title: PILA set to anchor Penang as regional logistics hub
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow described the Penang International Logistics Aeropark (PILA) as a game changer for the state's logistics and industrial landscape. During a visit to the project site, Chow said the strategic joint venture between the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) would anchor Penang's position as a regional logistics hub. Chow, who is also PDC chairman, said PILA would feature a new cargo terminal designed to strengthen the northern region's logistics ecosystem while supporting the continued expansion of Penang's industrial base.

items.title: Family seeks court order for murder probe into Teoh Beng Hock's death
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The family of Teoh Beng Hock has filed a fresh legal bid to compel police to investigate his death 17 years ago as murder. Beng Hock's brother Teoh Meng Kee and his parents Teoh Leong Hwee and Teoh Shuw Hoi filed the leave application to initiate a judicial review via Messrs Lim Wei Jiet last month. The applicants named the Inspector-General of Police, the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director, the police, the Attorney-General (A-G), and the government as respondents.

items.title: [UPDATED] Immigration 'game changer': New NIISe system to clear travellers in seconds at KLIA
items.summary: SEPANG: Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 1 is set for faster passenger processing with a new integrated digital clearance system capable of clearing travellers in four to five seconds. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the system, known as the National Integrated Immigration System (NIISe), will be implemented in phases and is expected to significantly ease congestion at both arrival and departure points. Also present were Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain and Home Ministry secretary general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman.

items.title: Energy crisis fuels calls to cut methane emissions
items.summary: PARIS: Officials pushed Monday for faster action to reduce methane emissions from the fossil fuel sector to both help slow climate change and boost energy security as the Middle East war chokes off supply. French Ecological Transition Minister Monique Barbut said she hoped the Paris meeting would help to "accelerate the implementation of effective solutions to reduce methane emissions." Around 60 per cent of methane emissions are linked to human actions.

items.title: RM700 million lost annually to illegal bitcoin mining power theft
items.summary: KUANTAN: Electricity theft linked to illegal bitcoin mining activities costs Malaysia an estimated RM700 million annually. Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the rampant illegal activities nationwide remain a major concern, with authorities stepping up enforcement against offenders. "Today, we raided two houses in Kuantan for stealing electricity for illegal bitcoin mining.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt to expand Jualan Rahmah Madani to 30,000 locations nationwide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will expand the Jualan Rahmah Madani programme to 30,000 locations nationwide this year as part of efforts to reduce the cost of living. "Last year, the government implemented more than 25,000 Jualan Rahmah programmes involving all state constituencies in the country. Anwar said efforts will also be made to ensure small traders benefit from the programme.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt-linked agencies to cut commercial rents to ease pressure on small traders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rental rates for commercial premises owned by government-linked agencies will be reduced to help ease the financial burden on small traders and businesses. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said agencies such as Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) and Uda Holdings Bhd will begin implementing the reductions this month, with details to be announced at the district level. He said the move is aimed at easing operational pressures faced by small traders, hawkers and food operators amid rising costs.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt moves to arm border personnel in high-risk areas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is moving towards arming personnel of the Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA), particularly those deployed in high-risk areas. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said a policy decision has been reached to equip the agency with weapons, with implementation to be overseen by the ministry's top leadership. Also present were Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, MCBA director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain and Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman.

items.title: Padi farmers to get RM200 advance payment to ease operating costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has agreed to provide a RM200 advance payment under the padi cultivation incentive scheme to help ease cash flow pressures faced by padi farmers. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move, approved at the National Economic Action Council meeting, is aimed at supporting padi farmers who are affected by rising operational costs, including land preparation and machinery rental. The initiative involves an allocation of RM48 million and is expected to benefit about 240,000 registered padi farmers nationwide.

items.title: [UPDATED] Home Ministry to expand Malaysian Border Guard intake this year
items.summary: SEPANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) will expand its manpower under the Malaysian Border Guard (MBG) initiative this year. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the agency has been given approval to recruit up to 200 border guards. Also present were Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, MCBA director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain and Home Ministry secretary general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman.

items.title: Umno to stay in Negeri Sembilan unity government, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Umno has no intention of becoming the opposition in Negeri Sembilan and will continue supporting and remain as part of the state’s Unity Government, party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. He said the party’s national leadership has finalised the position of all 14 Umno assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan to continue backing the existing state government. Umno at the national level has decided that all 14 assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan will remain part of the Unity Government,” he told reporters after the Kami Gen Mara Gathering here today.

items.title: Johor govt aims to fill remaining 59pc vacancies at Pasir Gudang Hospital by September
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 5 — The Johor government is aiming to fill the remaining 59 per cent of vacancies at Pasir Gudang Hospital (HPG) by September. “At present, 14 specialist clinics or clinical services at HPG are already fully operational. Last Thursday, Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail, in his speech at the state legislative assembly opening, decreed for HPG to be fully operational immediately.

items.title: Drunk driver remanded seven days in murder probe over fatal Jalan Ampang crash that killed married couple
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A fatal crash involving a married couple on Jalan Ampang last Saturday is now being investigated as murder. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the 24-year-old male suspect, who was previously receiving treatment at Hospital Kuala Lumpur, has been arrested to assist in the investigation, according to Kosmo! “Today, Kuala Lumpur Senior Assistant Registrar Nur Atikah Zakaria allowed a seven-day remand until May 11 to facilitate investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Cameron Highlands floods: TNB ordered to pay RM4.7m over 2013 dam negligence
items.summary: The High Court in Seremban made the award after assessing damages in the case, following earlier findings that TNB had acted negligently in managing the Sultan Abu Bakar Dam, according to Free Malaysia Today. Justice Ahmad Shahrir Salleh granted RM912,459.76 in special damages, alongside RM30,000 in general damages for each plaintiff. In 2018, the Court of Appeal upheld a High Court finding that TNB was negligent in maintaining the dam, and directed that damages be assessed.

items.title: Education Ministry fires back at MCA, says all 10A SPM students got matriculation offers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Education Ministry has refuted claims that several top SPM scorers were denied places in the matriculation programme, saying all eligible 10A students had already been offered admission, according to a Facebook post by Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh. Wong said MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong had recently alleged that the Madani government failed to fulfil its promise of offering Matriculation Programme places to 10A SPM high achievers, claiming he had received at least 11 cases of students allegedly left out. On May 4, Wong said Wee later claimed that all 11 students had secured places in the matriculation programme following MCA’s intervention.

items.title: Teoh Beng Hock murder probe: Family seeks court order for fresh investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The family of the late Teoh Beng Hock has filed a fresh application seeking to compel police to open a murder investigation into his death 17 years ago. He said the family is seeking a court declaration that Teoh’s death should be investigated as murder, as well as an order compelling police to complete the investigation within 120 days of the court order. Ramkarpal said the family had previously written to police asking whether any past investigation papers on Teoh’s death were opened for murder.

items.title: Man suspected of assaulting wife linked to burial of foetus in Muslim cemetery in Kedah, says police
items.summary: Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said investigations found that the 43-year-old suspect had confessed and led police to the burial site last Thursday, Harian Metro reported today. “Last Thursday, police brought the suspect together with his wife, the 39-year-old victim, to show the location where the foetus was buried. “We also brought a forensic team from the Kedah police headquarters to excavate the site, where we found an undeveloped foetal tissue,” he said at a press conference at the Kedah police headquarters today.

items.title: Batu Pahat kindergarten teacher fined RM1,200 for pinching four-year-old pupil until bruised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A kindergarten teacher in Batu Pahat has been fined RM1,200 after pleading guilty in the Yong Peng Magistrates’ Court for injuring a four-year-old child under her care by pinching the victim’s arm until it left bruises. Dissatisfied, the mother lodged a police report at Parit Sulong police station. Police later arrested the teacher at the kindergarten to assist investigations.

items.title: Kuala Krai crash: Two killed, two injured in Perodua Kembara and Honda City collision
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — Two people were killed and two others injured in a crash involving two vehicles near Simpang 3 Manek Urai Lama earlier this morning. He said the crash involved a Perodua Kembara and a Honda City. “The Honda City driver and a Perodua Kembara passenger were injured,” he said.

items.title: Man jailed three months, fined RM5,000 for insulting Yang di-Pertuan Agong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A man has been sentenced to three months’ jail and fined RM5,000 after pleading guilty at the Bandar Baru Bangi Sessions Court for making offensive remarks insulting the Yang di-Pertuan Agong in September last year. The accused, Mohd Hafiz Haron, 42, entered the plea after the charge was read to him before Judge Firdaus Md Isa, Harian Metro reported today. The court sentenced him to three months’ jail starting today and fined RM5,000, in default four months’ jail.

items.title: MARA Act amendment ready for Cabinet in three weeks, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A draft amendment to the 1966 Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) Act is set to be tabled before the Cabinet within three weeks, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. With the support and backing of MARA and senior officials, I will present the new MARA Act within three weeks from today. Also present was MARA chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
